exemplar.title: To what extent have Chipotle's CSR initiatives improved the company's reputation and financial performance following the 2015 E. coli outbreaks?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have Chipotle's CSR initiatives improved the company's reputation and financial performance following the 2015 E. coli outbreaks?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear and effective communication of the topic with comprehensive contextual explanation
Well-focused and explicitly stated research question guiding every section
Use of relevant conceptual definitions (CSR, ESG, Carrollâs Pyramid) to orient the reader
weaknesses: Occasional repetition and minor wording lapses in the methodology section
Lack of primary data limits the methodological depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates an accurate, effective explanation of the post-crisis CSR case, a succinct, well-focused research question, and an appropriate secondary-data methodology. Minor lapses in polish and absence of primary research justify placement at the lower end of the top band (5/6).

strengths: Mostly relevant and appropriate application of financial and CSR source material to support arguments
Clear and coherent subject knowledge of CSR theory and financial ratio analysis
Generally accurate use of terminology and frameworks
weaknesses: Over-reliance on corporate disclosures and Statista data weakens objectivity in places
Framework applications sometimes lack sufficient triangulation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate integrates quantitative tables and conceptual diagrams with clear understanding, but frequent factual slips, unit inconsistencies and dependence on corporate sources prevent a top-band award. The work sits squarely in the 3â4 band (4/6).

strengths: Coherent narrative linking crisis response to reputation recovery and financial rebound
Clear analytical thread using financial ratios and CSR frameworks with evidence-based interpretation
Thoughtful evaluation of methodological and causal limitations
weaknesses: Evaluation of alternative explanations remains somewhat superficial
Absence of primary data or statistical testing limits depth of causal claims
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation exhibits excellent research relevance, sophisticated analysis and a reasoned, critical argument. Minor unaddressed alternative factors and lack of primary data preclude a perfect 12, but the work otherwise meets the high-end descriptor for the 10â12 band, meriting 11/12.

strengths: Clearly appropriate overall structure following IB conventions with all required sections
Consistent use of headings, captions and in-text citations
Word count, title block and reference list correctly included
weaknesses: Some duplicated captions and occasional misaligned tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fully meets IB layout and structural requirements. Minor formatting inconsistencies and pagination oversights are present but do not materially impede readability, justifying a top-band award of 4/4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Clear statement** â The research question is prominently displayed in the title block and repeated in the introduction: âTo what extent have Chipotleâs CSR initiatives improved the companyâs reputation and financial performance following the 2015 E. coli outbreaks?â  It is succinct, time-bounded and specifies both dependent variables (reputation, financial performance).

**Well-focused linkage** â Every major section (CSR tools, financial ratios, ESG scores) feeds directly into evaluating either reputation or finance.  The question therefore governs discussion rather than appearing as an after-thought.

**Scope manageable yet challenging** â Although the dual focus is ambitious, the student narrows evidence to 2015-2022 and relies on Chipotle as single case study.  Minor drift appears when industry-wide trends are mentioned without depth, so a score of 5 (not 6) best reflects a strong but not flawless focus.

**Reasoned argument** â The essay develops a coherent narrative: crisis â CSR response â reputation recovery â financial rebound.  Each section feeds into the central thesis, culminating in a conclusion that revisits evidence.

**Critical evaluation** â The student repeatedly notes methodological and causal limitations, discusses the potential confounding effects of marketing and macro-economic trends, and critiques the simplicity of chosen frameworks, demonstrating emerging evaluative skill.

**Limitations** â Evaluation sometimes remains surface-level (e.g., brief treatment of alternative explanations) and the final judgement is rather sweeping.  Nonetheless, the argument is substantially developed and partially critical, meriting the 7-9 band with a score of 7.

**Clarity and coherence** â The student demonstrates solid knowledge of CSR theory, financial ratio analysis and business ethics.  Definitions are accurate and examples are current.

**Use of sources to build knowledge** â Academic concepts are supported with references, and quantitative evidence shows command of financial fundamentals such as NPM and OM.  Explanations of Chipotleâs operational changes (DNA testing, paid sick leave) display subject-specific insight.

**Limitations** â A few factual slips (e.g., claim that Chipotle paid â$282 billionâ in taxes) and some mis-calculations in tables signal less than complete mastery.  Overall knowledge is clear but not consistently rigorous enough for the 5-6 band.

**Terminology breadth** â Key Business Management termsâCSR, ESG, HACCP, net profit margin, operating margin, force-field analysisâare employed correctly and repeatedly.

**Conceptual accuracy** â The student generally applies frameworks appropriately (Carrollâs Pyramid levels, Lewinâs forces) and explains financial ratios with correct formulae.  Occasional minor inaccuracies (e.g., equating operating income with net profit in one table) prevent full consistency.

**Communication style** â Language is mostly precise and academic, though stylistic redundancies and minor grammatical slips occur.  The overall usage aligns with the expectations of the 3-4 bandâs âmostly accurateâ descriptor rather than the flawless consistency required for 5-6.

**Clear analytical thread** â Financial ratio calculations are used to trace performance pre- and post-crisis; CSR frameworks are applied to interpret strategic responses.  The student attempts to link cause (CSR initiatives) and effect (reputation, profit) and even challenges the causality by mentioning alternative factors (marketing campaigns, industry trends).

**Evidential support** â Tables and figures substantiate claims, and the student interprets percentage changes rather than merely stating numbers.  Limitations of each analytical tool are acknowledged, showing reflective thinking.

**Room for greater sophistication** â Analysis stops short of modelling or statistically testing causation, and some repetition dilutes depth.  Thus, work aligns with the lower end of the 7-9 band: âclear, well-supported conclusionsâ without reaching full sophistication.

**Mostly relevant application** â Financial tables, force-field scores and CSR framework diagrams are used to substantiate claims about performance and reputation.  Data from Statista, Macrotrends and Chipotle reports are integrated into narrative discussion.

**Effective but uneven integration** â Figures are referenced in text (e.g., Tables 1â3, Figures 1â2) and interpreted, demonstrating understanding rather than simple description.  However, some images are decorative (logo) or repeated alt-text, and a few assertions on consumer perception lack corroborating evidence.

**Why not top band?** â Occasional over-reliance on Chipotleâs own materials blurs objectivity, and linking ESG score directly to profit lacks robust triangulation.  The application remains mostly â not wholly â relevant and appropriate.

**Range and relevance** â Sources include company annual reports, sustainability reports, Statista data, major newspapers, and academic/business frameworks (Carroll, Lewin).  All are directly pertinent to CSR and Chipotleâs finances.

**Evidence of informed selection** â The student justifies choosing Carroll over Triple Bottom Line and Force-Field over SWOT, and acknowledges website bias while relying on audited financials for accuracy.  Limitations of secondary research are candidly discussed.

**Gaps preventing top-band award** â No primary data (surveys, interviews) or triangulation is attempted; citations occasionally rely on corporate PR rather than independent analysis.  Consequently, the methodology is appropriate and explained but lacks the breadth and rigour expected for the highest band.

**Breadth and pertinence of research** â The essay leverages a good mix of quantitative (annual reports, Statista, Sustainalytics) and qualitative (news articles, sustainability reports) sources, all tightly linked to the research question.

**Demonstrated selectivity** â The student explicitly assesses source reliability (acknowledging bias in corporate websites) and justifies methodological choices, indicating thoughtful curation.  The data set spans 2006-2022, allowing temporal comparison before and after the crisis.

**Why 7 and not higher?** â Absence of primary research and heavy dependence on secondary corporate disclosures limit originality and depth, precluding entry into the 10-12 band, yet the relevance and coverage exceed the expectations of the 4-6 band.

**Layout compliance** â Title page, section headings, tables, figures and reference list are provided.  In-text citations use a consistent parenthetical style, and the overall document appears within the 4 000-word limit.

**Formatting accuracy** â The table of contents is functional, and images/tables are captioned.  However, there are formatting inconsistencies: duplicate descriptive captions, occasional misaligned tables, and no clear evidence of page numbering within the supplied excerpt.

**Resulting judgment** â Most but not all IB formatting conventions are met with minor errors; thus a secure 3 within the 3-4 band is appropriate rather than the full 4.

**Clarity and completeness of topic communication** â The student introduces the 2015 E. coli crisis, outlines Chipotleâs CSR response and explicitly connects these points to reputation and financial performance. Background data (market share, revenue, crisis timeline) provide a solid frame for non-specialist readers.

**Effective explanation** â Key terms such as CSR, ESG, Carrollâs Pyramid and Force-Field Analysis are briefly defined before use, demonstrating an awareness that readers need conceptual orientation. Quantitative formulas (e.g., market-share equation) and narrative commentary show how each element relates to the guiding theme of recovery.

**Minor shortcomings** â Occasional repetition and wording lapses (e.g., duplicated sentences in the methodology section) slightly detract from polish, so the work fits comfortably at the lower end of the top band rather than the full 6.

**Logical organisation** â The report follows IB EE conventions: title page, table of contents, introduction, methodology, analysis sections, conclusion and references.  Headings and sub-headings align to the research question and assist reader navigation.

**Inclusion of required elements** â Word count, research question and illustrative visuals are present.  Pagination and section numbering appear in the table of contents (though actual page numbers are not visible in the extract, the intent is clear).

**Minor issues** â Some duplication of content and occasional mis-placement of images suggest editing lapses, but overall the structure meets all formal requirements; hence the top mark of the 3-4 band is awarded.

annotations.comment: The inclusion of the word count at the beginning meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to formal conventions.
The table of contents is clearly structured, listing all required sections with page references, aiding navigation and demonstrating compliance with IB conventions.
The absence of a visible page number in the footer interrupts the readerâs navigation; include consistent pagination across all pages.
The repeated sentence on force-field limitations reappears verbatim, indicating a proofreading oversight; remove duplication for flow.
The claim that growth drivers outweigh challenges is asserted without comparative metrics; provide evidence or benchmarks to substantiate this evaluative judgment.
The research question is clearly stated, well-focused and effectively connected to the investigationâs aim, guiding the structure of subsequent analysis.
The formula for market share is correctly presented; include a worked example substituting actual sales figures to demonstrate calculation of 5.08%.
The introduction of the financial analysis lacks a transition; consider adding a brief linking sentence to guide the reader into quantitative evaluation.
The projection of market value growth at 10.2% annually lacks critical assessment of the sourceâs methodology; discuss potential forecast biases or alternative scenarios.
The assertion on customer loyalty decline is plausible but would benefit from citation of consumer surveys or loyalty metrics to substantiate the reputational impact.
The introduction effectively reiterates the research question, reinforcing its central role in shaping the essayâs scope and ensuring focus on CSRâs impact.

The methodology outlines secondary data sources without explaining why primary research (e.g., interviews or surveys) was not pursued; consider justifying this choice or acknowledging its effect on depth of insight.

The justification for selecting Carrollâs pyramid and force-field analysis is clear, but the discussion of their limitations remains superficial; elaborating on how these frameworks may skew analysis would strengthen the methodology.
The paragraph repeats the limitation of force-field analysis twice in succession, suggesting the need for tighter editing to avoid redundancy and improve clarity.

While acknowledging limitations of secondary data, the study stops short of proposing concrete primary-data methods; specifying potential survey or interview designs would enhance methodological rigor.
The scoring scale for the force-field analysis is introduced without clear criteria for assigning scores; define how each numeric value corresponds to qualitative impact for transparency.

The force-field diagram identifies drivers and restraints but does not link scores to quantitative performance outcomes; correlating these forces with financial indicators would deepen analytical depth.
The absence of a page number in the footer interrupts the readerâs navigation; include consistent pagination across all pages.
Table 1âs row labeled âRevenue ($US Millions)â contains no data; populate or remove the row to align table content with headings.
While NPM values look consistent, perform a sensitivity check to assess how rounding in financial data might alter calculated margins to validate robustness.
The stated figure of â$282 billionâ in taxes appears to be a unit error (likely million); verify and correct to maintain accuracy in financial reporting.
The entry âmore than 1000,000 peopleâ is formatted incorrectly; revise to â1,000,000â for numeric clarity and consistency.
The suggestion that children funded by CSR share positive word-of-mouth is plausible but should be supported by consumer perception data or secondary studies.
The critique of Carrollâs pyramid oversimplification is well noted but repeated; consolidate this evaluation to avoid redundancy and sharpen focus.
Describing Hal King as an âexpertâ on food safety should be accompanied by credentials and an assessment of his objectivity to validate his authoritative role.
The final sentence on the Wellness Check rule is incomplete; review and complete the description to ensure clarity of procedural detail.
The revenue chart is informative but omits axis labels and units; include clear labels (e.g., âRevenue (USD billions)â) for precise interpretation.
The concluding sentence introduces fresh claims on brand loyalty and growth; ensure the conclusion synthesizes analysis rather than presenting new findings.
The references list exhibits inconsistent citation formatting; apply a uniform style (e.g., APA or MLA) to ensure presentation consistency.
The embedded URL is broken across lines, disrupting readability; ensure links remain intact or use hyperlink text for clean formatting.
Entries after the references appear supplemental and resemble extended bibliography items; ensure only sources cited in the main text are listed to maintain focus and comply with IB requirements.
The average restaurant revenue figures are referenced without sourcing; include data source or footnote to substantiate these average metrics.
The NPM formula is correctly formatted, but clarify whether sales revenue and net profit refer to figures in thousands or millions to avoid unit inconsistencies.
The discussion implies a direct causal link between CSR efforts and NPM recovery; consider alternative explanations (e.g., marketing campaigns, macroeconomic factors) to contextualize findings.
The styling of the OM section header is inconsistent (bold and underline usage differs from previous headers); standardize heading formats throughout.
The caption â2015-2018 Operating Incomeâ¦â conflicts with table data spanning 2015â2022; update the caption to reflect the actual year range.
The OM formula in the table uses Net Profit instead of Operating Income; correct the formula to $$OM = \frac{Operating\ Income}{Sales\ Revenue} \times 100\%$$.
The narrative correlates high ESG scores with profit spikes but lacks statistical analysis; consider regression or correlation analysis to substantiate this relationship.
The explanation omits governance metrics despite their inclusion in the total ESG score; include governance-specific data to complete the framework assessment.
The summary of social criteria reads descriptively; enhance critical evaluation by discussing potential trade-offs or challenges in sourcing and employee satisfaction.
The conclusion restates analysis without deeper reflection on methodology limitations; include an evaluative summary of how secondary data reliance impacted the studyâs conclusions.
The recommendation for future research remains broad; propose specific research designs (e.g., longitudinal consumer surveys, focus groups) to address identified gaps.
